sections.paragraph:
συστέλλω 1 aor. συνέστειλα. Pass. 2 aor. 3 sg. συνεστάλη LXX, ptc. gen. pl. συσταλέντων GrBar 9:1; pf. ptc. συνεσταλμένος (Eur., Thu. et al.; ins, pap, LXX, Philo, Joseph.)

① to draw together so as to be less extended, limit, shorten (Hippocr.: CMG I/1 p. 53, 14; 85, 9; Isocr. 12, 230; X., Vect. 4, 3; Diod S 1, 41, 2; Cass. Dio 39, 37; Jos., Ant. 14, 74) of time ὁ καιρὸς συνεσταλμένος ἐστίν 1 Cor 7:29, where it is not certain whether Paul has a divine act of shortening in mind (JWeiss), or whether there is no reference intended to a time that was originally longer (Diod S 4, 20, 1 τοῖς ὄγκοις συνεσταλμένοι=compact in body [of the Ligurians]). ς. τὰ ἱστία shorten/furl the sails Ac 27:15 v.l., see s.v. ἱστίον.

② to limit the area of one’s presence, withdraw ἐγὼ … συνέστελλον ἑαυτὸν ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ I withdrew into the wilderness GJs 25:1.

③ to wrap up by winding someth. around, cover, wrap up (Eur., Tro. 377; Lucian, Imag. 7; Achilles Tat. 8, 1, 5; Ps.-Callisth. 2, 22, 3 ὸ̔ν [=Darius when dying] τῇ χλαμύδι συστείλας). So the Syrian and Coptic versions; many comm.; NRSV et al. for the rendering of οἱ νεώτεροι συνέστειλαν αὐτόν Ac 5:6; cp. vs. 10 D. Held less in favor are mngs. 4 and 5.

④ to gather up, pack, fold up, snatch up (cp. GrBar 8:2 ὄρνεον … συστέλλον τὰς πτερύγας αὐτοῦ bird … folding its wings; Psellus 50, 31 ς. τὰ παραπετάσματα=gather the curtains together). So the Armenian version and HMeyer, Overbeck, Weizsäcker, Zahn for Ac 5:6 (s. 3 and 5).

⑤ to remove an object from a place, take away, remove (Philo, Leg. All. 3, 35). So the Vulgate, amoverunt for Ac 5:6 (but s. 3 and 4).—M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ptc. ptc. = participle

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. pl. = plural

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

  1. pf. = perfect

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

CMG CMG = Corpus Medicorum Graecorum—List 5

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

someth. someth. = something

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Achilles Tat Achilles Tat , IV a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

comm. comm. = commentary, -aries, commentator(s)

NRSV NRSV = New Revised Standard Version of the NT—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Psellus Psellus , History, XI a.d.—List 5

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 978
